AnomanderDeLuna: Anomander's birthday is on the 12th of Eleasis (Highsun), 1476. He is 19 years old.
Anomander did not have many friends before he met the adventuring group. His one and only friend from his childhood was killed when he was sent to fight a dragon.
Anomander was trained as a swordsman by the head of his family's guard, Master Lennit.

While training as a swordsman, Anomander was quite nervous in combat. His master taught him a breathing technique to focus his mind and strip away emotion while fighting an enemy. He often employed this technique in his early days of adventuring.
Anomander has become more comfortable with combat and relies less on his breathing techniques to focus on a fight, allowing him to be more aware of the wider field of battle.
Anomander is a noble from the aristocratic DeLuna family from Waterdeep. After his best friend's death, he ran away from home to live out their shared dream to become adventurers.
Whether alone or with Luo and Bolo, Anomander trains every morning. He goes through the various sword forms as a Kata exercise.
Anomander is naive and doesn't know much about the way of the world. Everything he does know comes from stories he read as a child. He's learning on the job with his adventuring companions.
Anomander is an Aasimar. Due to his connection to a Deva, Kidalael, he is able to summon echoes of himself from potential would-be timelines.
Anomander is not good at being subtle, and can't always read the room well.
Anomander is somewhat proficient in smithing, having spent some time with a blacksmith.
Anomander forged his own swords: Courage and Brave Soul.
Anomander has no concept of money.
Anomander likes grand and heroic names and titles, such as “He Who Comes with the Dawn”, which is, of course, the name of his horse.
Anomander experienced a whole other life while in the Whispering Planes.
BoloBonestaff: Bolo's birthday is on the 18th of Ches (The Claw of the Sunsets), 1472. He is 23 years old.
Bolo is a renowned sorcerer and adventurer; graduating top of his class at Kobold Kollege, hero of many towns, and having invented multiple spells. [citation needed]
Bolo has a dark and mysterious past.
Bolo has a horse named Hoarse.
When you met Bolo, he was 3'. Last publicly available record shows that he is now 3' 10". They grow up so fast.
Bolo has many siblings. Spurt, Yip, Sivel, Deekin, Bex... he could go on.
Bolo fought in the Great Battle of the Battlements of the High Forest as part of the 666th Kobold infantry regiment. [citation needed]
Bolo has never been arrested. [citation needed]
Bolo is less than pleased with the state of his arm and would very much like to speak with management.

Bolo defended Karse village against an onslaught of rabid Owl Bears. [citation needed] Through thorough investigation and a sharp wit, he learned that they were commanded by a legion of Mind Flayers, and swiftly dispatched of them too. [citation needed] What do ya mean citation needed? I'm citing it right here. I'll citate you c'mere.
Along with mastering the arcane arts, Bolo is also an accomplished lawyer and doctor. [citation needed]

Bolo spends the mornings training or meditating with Luo and Anomander. He has taken the charge of being Luo's and Anomander's current mentor, and teaches them unique techniques and fighting styles, or has them do ridiculous exercises.
Have you ever seen Bolo and Gaiar in the same room at the same time?
Before joining the party, Bolo's previous adventuring party consisted of Roblin the Goblin, Tony the tome seller, and his best friend, Caleb Woods.

Bolo got his curse when, in a dire situation, he pulled a card from a deck belonging to one of his companions. He was shunted somewhere else for a moment, and next thing he knew, he had a cursed arm and was in the middle of nowhere.
While not wearing armour, Bolo's cursed arm is always bandaged up.
[citation needed]
In recent times, Bolo has begun teaching sorcery in the Neverwinter Academy, which includes teaching Elle's sister. He taught 'Introduction to Sorcery' and his students call him Professor Bolo.
Bolo spent some of his spare time in Neverwinter on artificing projects with Zigor, as well as learning about runic applications to magic and combat.
Calder taught Bolo how to read and speak Giant.
CalderThane: Calder's birthday is on the 24th of Ches (The Claw of the Sunsets), 1470. He is 25 years old.
Calder carries a well-kept pocket watch with his family crest on it. He is sure to wind it every night.
Wedge personally chose to be an owl, so Calder doesn't transform Wedge into any other type of animal.
When Calder wasn't reading books or writing something in his journal, he used to write letters and throw them away. That was until he met his sister again in Neverwinter.
Did you notice that Calder always takes Elle's side?

Calder was apprehensive to join the group at first, and remained detached for a time. Ever since Neverwinter, he has grown more comfortable with adventuring, and has become fully invested with the group.
Calder has a horse named Russa II.

Calder suffers from an a̴̛̤͓̓̇̋̓̎̐̄̄̑͋̃̎ͅf̸̢̜̥̗̙̣̲̩͎̼͙̲̜̩̏͌͂̃͗͑̒͂̏͂͝f̶̩̥̤̞̂͝l̵̛̠̳͎̟̝̂͂͋̅͒̋́̾̾͆̔͘͝͝i̴̮̠͎̩̮̪̯͓̔̓͂̈́͜c̴̢̱̲̩̩͉̩͕̬̾ͅţ̸̛̙͇͓͆͒̇̿̓̉́͝i̷̘̙̠̤̟͓̫͍̬̘̯̪̞̋̑͊̄̍̈́̌͠ǫ̶̧̢̢̙̻̟͈͖͓͍̣̱͍̔̏̓̋̍̈́̓̚n̴̡͇̫̠̗̤͌͐͌ that makes him appear undead. He has suffered from it since the ruins of the Netherese city of Hlondath vanished, leaving him behind.
The party met Calder when they rescued him from a group of slavers. By chance, Calder had heard of Bolo through their mutual friend, Caleb Woods.
EllethaCarlamore: Elle's birthday is on the 28th of Hammer (Deepwinter). No one knows how old she is. Not even the gods.
Elle has a sweet tooth. Peach drops are her favourite.
Elle has a horse named Cherry.
Elle always sits in chairs in strange positions. She'll generally opt to stand though.
Elle learned to use a new alias in each city she visits. She originally started doing this to protect her family.

Elle's sisters are the most important people in the world to her. Don't mess with her family. You will die. You won't know where, when, or how. She will find you. She will reach you. You cannot stop her. You will live in terror until she decides it is time.
Leza has started learning magic and wants to study it. Fey will fight you.
Elle can tell the future through Tarot.

Elle prefers to approach situations with stealth and subterfuge. To do this, she has learned to use poisons effectively and will tend to scout ahead using a form of clairvoyance. Reading minds helps, too.
Elle was willing to die on Oman's Isle to take out those who threatened her family. Now that they're gone, that fear of death has been finding its way back into her life.
Much like Calder, Elle can almost always be found reading in her spare time. Half the party's carry capacity is probably books.
Elle can hurt you by looking at you.
Elle can walk through spacetime. And she brought friends.
The party met Elletha Carlamore under the alias of Rian Nightshade, one of many. She was investigating a man named Elvron Hilrosen, owner of Alluring Artifacts.
Gaiar: Gaiar's birthday is on the 21st of Tarsakh (The Claw of Storms), 1466. He is 29 years old.

Gaiar has a fixation on repairing damaged or broken things, to a point that it would cloud his judgement and thought process. He holds onto many seemingly useless sundries in case they could be useful in fixing something.
Gaiar recently learned that he has a fear of confined spaces, something that he has little experience of as an Aarakocra from the Elemental Plane of Air.
Gaiar was born in the Elemental Plane of Air amongst the Howling Gyre. He has 2 elder brothers, 2 elder sisters, and a younger sister.
Gaiar once served as a protector against the threats to the Elemental Plane of Air. His people protected the Vaati.

H'aara-shie, translated as Cloud Chasing, is a sport played by Aarakocra. It celebrates legends that the Aarakocra were created as storm herders. Once upon a time Gaiar was considered to be very good at it.
Gaiar came to the material plane through a portal which connects the Elemental Plane of Air and the Mistcliffs of Chult.
Gaiar injured one of his wings during an attack on his home in the middle of a H'aara-shie competition. He never fully recovered, which impacted his ability to fly.

Gaiar spent a number of years in the material plane crewing ships sailing off the coasts of Chult. One ill-fated voyage through the Trackless Sea ended with something ripping through his ship, and he seemingly drowned alongside the rest of his crew.

Many years ago, after Gaiar's ship was attacked, he awoke in Nimbral with only vague memories of what happened in between. He was rescued by his now lifelong friend, Valia Yalti, who equipped him with a mechanical mask that helps him breathe and speak.
Gaiar was tasked by Valia to investigate Vrilosh and Tilsholo. He met the party on the Ocean's Lament and joined them as their goals seemed to coincide.
The party met Gaiar on the Ocean's Lament, an Aarakocra who wears a mechanical mask that helps him breathe and speak.
ImmeralMeliamne: Immeral Meliamne is an Eladrin who supposedly appears randomly in places that he's needed. He assisted the party in defeating the red dragon Cinderblast, before promptly disappearing as quickly as he appeared.
LuoKrillki: Luo's birthday is on the 1st of Tarsakh (The Claw of Storms), 1476. He is 19 years old.
Before Luo met Visk and the rest of the group, he had spent the past few years in isolation as a hermit in the forests south of Yartar, foraging off the land.
Before Luo arrived in the Sword Coast, he spent his early years in Chult where he was trained by his mentor, Daskel.
Luo was transported to the sword coast when his mentor gave him a set of scrolls before he was murdered by shadowy figures. Luo hasn't returned since, and still does not know the fate of his home.
Luo's scrolls are an artifact passed down by the Grif'tol Karem. They absorb knowledge from the experiences of their owner.

Luo's duty is to protect the scrolls in his charge, and to experience the world in order to grow the scroll's knowledge. His mentor was the previous owner, and the scrolls seem to be linked to his mentor's death.
Luo adventures with the party to explore the world, and to protect the people he cares most about. He has a strong desire to protect and help people whenever possible.
Luo spends most of his spare time in meditation and in nature. He also enjoys training with other party members, and playing dragon chess in the evenings.
Luo has a horse named Shadow Foot.
During Yartar's Midsummer festival, Luo met an Air Genasi named Braefil Glatomeerio. He claimed to be student of his late master, Daskel.
The five scrolls given to Luo are legendary artifacts known as the Realm Scrolls.
RayMercer: Ray's birthday is on the 12th of Flamerule (Summertide). Few know how old he is, though Luo is aware, having seen Ray's grave.
Ray has a horse named Benji.

Ray travelled the best part of the Sword Coast, joining many groups, but often falling out due to one problem or another. He met his first trusted group in Baldur's Gate, becoming friends with Exel Lighthold, Landon Quillbit and Fortan.

Ray and his old adventuring group took a high paying contract from a merchant in Iriaebor where they were to hunt down an artifact. This led them to an old ruin in The Sword in Anauroch, where Ray's companions met their untimely demise.

Ray has passed through more places in Faerûn than most. He has stories to tell from regions such as Anauroch, or from towns along the Sword Coast, including Secomber, Red Larch, Baldur's Gate and Candlekeep, to name a few.
After escaping an attempt on his life and the demise of his family, Ray ended up at Red Larch. It was here that he took up the mantle of Ray Mercer.
Ray used to work at Vallivoe's Sundries in Red Larch. When he had lost everything, he needed to earn enough money to travel the Sword Coast. Vallivoe sponsored his journey with discounts on supplies.
Ray likes to talk about rare and powerful artifacts dotted around the Sword Coast.
Ray was born to the aristocratic Sah family in Waterdeep.
Ray's ancestors heralded from the far away land of Maztica. They built the Iron Keep in Oman's Isle.
Ray spent some time in Nimbral learning the basics of artificing with his aunt, Valia.
Snufkin: Snufkin was killed by Gnolls while on his first job. Bolo brought him back to life with his curse, though Snufkin was not the same and Bolo's curse spread further.
Visk: Visk's birthday is... unknown. He told people that 64 seasons had passed.
Visk is buried in the woods behind the party's house in Yartar. A message is carved into a tree by his grave: “Here lies one of Her Wardens, whose life was sadly needed to balance a moment in time”.
Visk was originally from a tribe in the Mere of Dead Men. He left because of his clan's reluctance to heed his warnings about trading with the Cult of the Dragon.
Visk was good at making things. He often could be found whittling little trinkets from bone, or jury-rigging alarm traps.
Visk was the runt of his siblings, and learned from a young age to create traps to catch small game instead of using brute force.
Visk's first friend after leaving his clan was Luo. He was cautious at first, but they became fast friends sharing a love of nature.
Visk worshiped the Great Dirt Ghost. He claimed this entity to be the most powerful force of nature. No one else has heard of them.
Visk is twitchy, and panics in moments of danger. "Better safe than dead!"
When asked what his religious views were, Visk responded with: "The Great Dirt Ghost". When asked what his political views were, he responded with: "The Great Dirt Ghost".
Visk's answer to most things was to dig out a covered trench using the Great Dirt Ghost's magic, and hide in it. Visk's answer to anything else was usually to hit it with a stick.

Visk had a limp on his right side that disappeared when fighting. It was explained that when he was little, he narrowly escaped an encounter with a crocodile. There's no visible scar, and this was likely psychosomatic.
General: Session 0 of the campaign was on the 24th of March in 2020, and the campaign started on the 3rd of April.

https://app.syrinscape.com/d5107f81626f4b259d4377566b8c3ba1/player/
The party's full name is “What is the Right Thing? The Royal Heroes of the World.” The name turns heads throughout the Sword Coast.

The party met in the town of Yartar during Yartar's renowned Hiring Fair, which preceded the Midsummer Festival. They got a job at The Smiling Company adventurer's guild to deal with some Gnolls that were harrying travellers on the roads.

The Great Dirt Ghost had told Visk that something was wrong with the land to the south of Yartar. The party investigated it, defeating a strange creature with a crystal, but inadvertently causing its lair to collapse, killing a family of three.

The party took a job for Faltir Swiftpaw, owner of Trinkets and Baubles, to find the moving location known as the Tomb of Heroes. After finding the tomb, they overcame the Trial of Heroes, coming away with some new boons.

Meldu Cal is a talking tree, who claims to be a fairy from the Feywild. Bolo agreed to help it return to its original form, on the condition that it tells the story of “What is the Right Thing? The Royal Heroes of the World”. Bolo carries a twig belonging to the tree.

When Anomander was approached by two bounty hunters in Yartar, hired by his father, a series of chaotic events unfolded which led to the deaths of Visk and Snufkin. The party enlisted the assistance of Tymora Greenleaf to help them escape the city.
The Vrael Olo are a Yuan-ti cult, whose name translates as 'Favoured Ones'.

The party was gifted a house in Yartar when they foiled an assassination attempt by the Vrael Olo on Nestra Ruthiol, the Waterbaron of Yartar. This house previously belonged to The Wind Wardens, an adventuring group that disappeared 40 years ago.
The Wind Wardens consisted of: Aleth, a dragonborn; Balen and Balia, a pair of half elven twins; Gothar Hilsword, a dwarf; Francesca, a halfling; and Amelia, a human.
The party hired Anomander's old friend, who was servant of his family, and a father figure, Kinn Valefor, to look after their house while they were away. He is paid very well.

Waterdeep had been terrorised for years by a red dragon known as Cinderblast. When the Undercliff was attacked by Kobolds and Lizardfolk who served the dragon, the party split in heated debate. Bolo and Anomander left to speak with the attacking tribe.
The Kobolds and Lizardfolk who served Cinderblast now live peacefully with the people of the Undercliff outside the walls of Waterdeep, thanks to the political efforts lead by Bolo.
The party liberated Oman's Isle from the Iron Fist, getting revenge for Elle and her family. The leader of the Iron Fist, Immek, turned out to be a Yuan-ti named Natszish.
The Vrael Olo Va Zehir, who opposed the Vrael Olo, attempted to replicate the Heaven's Key staff using Dunamanium ore. They failed, only creating a strange stone known as the Fragment of Heaven.
Ron Greenleaf, father of Tymora Greenleaf, was rescued from Oman's Isle. He was previously thought to be dead.

Strange events have been happening all throughout the Sword Coast. Places have been disappearing, fauna are acting strange, and unnatural creatures have been appearing. The most noteworthy events are the disappearance of Candlekeep, and the reopening of the chasm in Neverwinter.
The party has closed rifts in the lair of Cinderblast and in the chasm of Neverwinter.
Tilsholo seems to be a leader in the Vrael Olo, and is trying to use the Heaven's Key staff to free Sseth, a yuan-ti god, who disappeared centuries ago.
Toril used to be a place called Abeir-Toril, until it was sunderered by the Overgod Ao, into two worlds, Abeir, and Toril.
Caleb, Bolo's friend, is seemingly trapped on Abeir, and holds one half of Bolo's curse.

The Netherese empire ended when a mage known as Karthus created a spell which came to be known as Karthus' Folly. He killed Mystra to become a god, but caused magic to be undone, and the flying cities of the Netherese empire came crashing to the ground and oceans below.
The Time of the Troubles occurred a few centuries ago, a period where the gods walked as mortals in the material realm. Many were killed during this time.
The Spellplague was an event that was caused by the murder of the incarnation of Mystra around the time of the Troubles, causing magic to become wild throughout Toril and Abeir.
